description: This file contains the complete Balanda pronoun system, including personal pronouns, inclusive/exclusive distinctions, object usage, possessive constructions, reflexive forms, plural forms, and the impersonal pronoun. Pronouns in Balanda are morphologically simple but semantically and pragmatically rich.

person_number_system.structure: {"first_person": {"singular": {"form": "Ne", "meaning": "I / me"}, "plural": {"exclusive": {"form": "Dhuu", "meaning": "We (excluding the listener)"}, "inclusive": {"form": "Danda", "meaning": "We (including the listener)"}}}, "second_person": {"singular": {"form": "Ŋo", "meaning": "You (singular)"}, "plural": {"form": "Dha", "meaning": "You all"}}, "third_person": {"singular": {"form": "Ni", "meaning": "He / She"}, "plural": {"form": "Ndi", "meaning": "They"}}}

impersonal_pronoun.structure: {"form": "Ay", "reference": "non-human, non-animal entities only", "semantic_scope": ["inanimate objects", "substances", "materials", "abstract or unspecified entities"], "exclusions": ["humans", "animals"], "examples": ["Ay no muru dühü (It is on the rock)", "Ŋo baha ay (You see it)", "Vara ndo’o no kono (Its oil is here)", "Ay tingoko to’o (It moves itself)"]}
impersonal_pronoun.notes: Ay | non-human, non-animal entities only | inanimate objects; substances; materials; abstract or unspecified entities | humans; animals | Ay no muru dühü (It is on the rock); Ŋo baha ay (You see it); Vara ndo’o no kono (Its oil is here); Ay tingoko to’o (It moves itself)

syntactic_roles.structure: {"subject": "Pronouns may function as sentence subjects", "object": "The same pronoun forms are used as objects without case change", "example": {"subject": "Ne mande gbi Wau", "object": "Ŋo baha ne"}}
syntactic_roles.notes: Pronouns may function as sentence subjects | The same pronoun forms are used as objects without case change

possessive_constructions.structure: {"possessive_adjective": {"structure": "NOUN + ndi-PRONOUN", "example": "Joko ndi-ne (my bag)"}, "possessive_pronoun": {"structure": "ngandi-PRONOUN", "example": "Vili no ngandi-danda (The money is ours)"}}

reflexive_pronouns.structure: {"formation": {"structure": "ti-PRONOUN", "function": "Indicates that the subject acts on itself"}, "examples": ["Ne merego ti-ne (I love myself)", "Dhuu yirisa ti-dhuu (We respect ourselves, exclusive)", "Danda yirisa ti-danda (We respect ourselves, inclusive)", "Ndi merego ti-ndi (They love themselves)", "Ay tingoko to’o (It moves itself)"]}
reflexive_pronouns.notes: Ne merego ti-ne (I love myself); Dhuu yirisa ti-dhuu (We respect ourselves, exclusive); Danda yirisa ti-danda (We respect ourselves, inclusive); Ndi merego ti-ndi (They love themselves); Ay tingoko to’o (It moves itself)

inclusive_exclusive_rule.structure: {"dhuu": "Must be used when the listener is excluded", "danda": "Must be used when the listener is included", "constraint": "Inclusive and exclusive forms are never interchangeable"}
inclusive_exclusive_rule.notes: Must be used when the listener is excluded | Must be used when the listener is included | Inclusive and exclusive forms are never interchangeable

agreement_rules.structure: {"verbs": "Verbs do not change form based on pronouns", "copula": "Copula 'no' does not agree with pronouns"}
agreement_rules.notes: Verbs do not change form based on pronouns | Copula 'no' does not agree with pronouns

question_and_negation.structure: {"questions": "Formed by adding sentence-final 'we?'", "negation": "Formed by adding clause-final 'gba'"}
question_and_negation.notes: Formed by adding sentence-final 'we?' | Formed by adding clause-final 'gba'

constraints.structure: Pronouns are not inflected for case or gender; Inclusive and exclusive 'we' must be semantically respected; Reflexive meaning must use ti-PRONOUN, not bare pronouns; Possessive meaning must use ndi- or ngandi-, not bare pronouns; Pronouns must not take verb prefixes (A-, ti-, le-); Impersonal pronoun 'ay' must never refer to humans or animals; Animals must use 'Ni' (singular) or 'Ndi' (plural), not 'ay'

output: First-person singular pronoun is Ne (I/me). First-person plural has two forms: Dhuu (exclusive we) and Danda (inclusive we).
Second-person singular pronoun is Ŋo (you). Second-person plural pronoun is Dha (you all).
Third-person singular pronoun is Ni (he/she/it). Third-person plural pronoun is Ndi (they).

instruction: Explain inclusive vs exclusive 'we' in Balanda
output: Dhuu is exclusive we (excluding the listener). Danda is inclusive we (including the listener).
Incorrect reflexive form. Correct form: Ne merego ti-ne.
Incorrect usage. Use Dhuu for exclusive meaning: Dhuu mande gbi Wau.
Incorrect word order. Correct form: Ŋo baha ai.
